_doc: Calibrated selectors for skills/adp_run_automation/compensation_backend.py against ADP RUN's Reports > My saved custom reports > 'Earnings and Hours V1' report. Produces an .xlsx with one row per paycheck-earning-line, from which we infer current wage rate per employee.
verified_by: Cursor AI agent driving user-playwright MCP, fresh from-scratch flow against Palmetto Superfoods account.
discovery_path: Originally planned to scrape Employees > Compensation (per-employee detail page). Would have required iterating ~12-14 employees x 1 page each.
User redirected: Reports > scroll to 'My saved custom reports' > 'Earnings and Hours V1' custom report.
The custom report opens the 'Custom report builder' modal: date range picker, employment-status filter, employees filter (defaults to All), report name/description.

Setting Custom date range + clicking 'Preview report' renders a paginated grid (subset only). To get ALL rows we click 'Download' > 'Excel (.xlsx)'. ADP then queues generation (~3-10s) and shows a 'Your report is ready to download' dialog with a 'Download report' button.
Generated reports are also archived for 30 days under Reports > Generated custom reports (useful as a fallback if the daily generation fails).
report_portability_caveat: 'Earnings and Hours V1' is a SAVED CUSTOM REPORT in the Palmetto account, not a system standard report. Other stores onboarding to BHAGA must create the equivalent before this backend will work.

Custom-report column composition (used by parser): Employee Name, Payroll Check Date, Period Start Date, Period End Date, Payroll Earning Hours, Payroll Hourly Earning Rate, Payroll Earning Amount, Payroll Earning Description.

Creation flow (manual, one-time per store): Reports > '+ Create custom report' (TBD calibration). Until that flow is documented, treat the report as a per-store prerequisite captured in the store profile.

login.notes: Same flow as Timecard report -- see selectors/timecards.json#login. Reuses adp_{store}_login Keychain credential.

via_my_saved_custom_reports: Reports page renders categories. Scroll down to 'My saved custom reports' section.
Click the row/link labeled 'Earnings and Hours V1' (or whatever the store's saved report is called -- read from store profile key `adp_wage_rate_report_name`).
Opens the 'Custom report builder' dialog with prior filters pre-populated (likely a date range from the last run -- always overwrite explicitly).

custom_report_builder_modal.modal_dialog_indicator: dialog with banner heading 'Custom report builder', subheading '{Company Name}'
custom_report_builder_modal.no_iframe: Unlike the Timecard modal, the Custom report builder runs in the main page DOM. No iframe traversal required.

date_range_preset_combobox.combobox_aria_pattern: Date range {Custom date range|This week|Last week|...}
date_range_preset_combobox.notes: When 'Custom date range' is selected, the From/To textboxes appear below. ADP enforces 'Can be a max of 12 months' on the range.

from_date_textbox.notes: Type-and-blur works (fill() sets value then implicit change event). The adjacent 'Choose a date.' button opens a calendar widget that's harder to drive.

employment_status_combobox.notes: Leave at Active for daily refresh. If you ever need historical wage data for terminated employees, switch to 'All'.

people_in_report_radio.notes: Always 'All' for BHAGA -- we want every employee's current rate.

preview_report_button.notes: Renders a TRUNCATED preview grid in the modal (subset of rows only). Does NOT replace the Download flow.

download_button.notes: Dropdown trigger. Expanding it reveals a 2-item menu: 'Excel (.xlsx)' and 'PDF (.pdf)'. Use Excel.

download_menu_excel_option.notes: Clicking does NOT trigger an immediate download. ADP queues report generation and opens a 'Your report is ready to download' confirmation dialog when generation finishes (~3-10s).

download_menu_pdf_option.notes: Do NOT use -- parser only handles xlsx.

ready_to_download_dialog.dialog_aria_name: Your report is ready to download

download_report_button.notes: Triggers the actual file save. File arrives at extracted/downloads/Earnings-and-Hours-V1.xlsx.

download_later_button.notes: Closes the dialog without downloading. The file remains queued in Reports > Generated custom reports for 30 days.

xlsx_format.filename_pattern: Earnings and Hours V1.xlsx (or the saved-report name verbatim)

metadata_rows.shape: Rows 1-5 are metadata (Report title, Company, IID, DateRange, Generated timestamp). Row 6 is the column header. Rows 7+ are data.
rows: Row 1: Report title (e.g. 'Earnings and Hours V1')
Row 2: 'Company: {LEGAL ENTITY NAME}' (e.g. AK JUICY BOWLS LLC for Palmetto)
Row 4: 'DateRange : M/D/YYYY to M/D/YYYY' (note the space-colon-space before the range)

xlsx_format.employee_name_sparseness: Employee Name is populated ONLY on the first row of each employee's block. Subsequent rows for the same employee leave it blank. Parser MUST forward-fill.
xlsx_format.date_format: openpyxl returns these as datetime.datetime at midnight (sheet stores them as Excel date serials).
xlsx_format.hours_format: DECIMAL hours (e.g. 30.18 means 30 hours 11 minutes). Distinct from the Timecard XLSX which uses 'H:MM' notation.
xlsx_format.rate_format: Decimal dollars per hour (e.g. 15.25). Cell is formatted '$15.2500' in the UI but openpyxl returns the raw 15.25.
xlsx_format.amount_format: Decimal dollars. Negative values appear on reversal/void rows.
description_taxonomy_calibrated_2026_05_: Credit Card Tips Owed -- tips paid through paycheck (amount only)
Cash tips -- TAX TRACKING ONLY. Always appears as a +$X and -$X pair on the same check that nets to $0. The actual cash was paid out at the shop; this is for IRS reporting only.

Misc reimbursement non-taxable -- the actual disbursement equivalent of the cash tips on that paycheck (paid out as a non-taxable reimbursement so the employee gets the dollars without double-taxation).
xlsx_format.reversal_pattern: Voided/reissued checks emit a triplet on the same Check Date: ($X) + (-$X) + ($X) for the same Description (often 'Regular'). The parser preserves all three rows; downstream callers can net them out by summing per (employee, check_date, description) if needed. For wage-rate inference this doesn't matter -- we filter to positive-hours Regular rows.

wage_rate_inference_rules._doc: How compensation_backend.py converts the per-line earnings table into one wage rate per employee.
wage_rate_inference_rules.step1_filter_to_hourly_rows: Keep rows where Description in {'Regular', 'Overtime'} AND Hours > 0 AND Hourly Earning Rate > 0.
wage_rate_inference_rules.step2_separate_overtime_rate: Overtime rate is typically 1.5x Regular. We track BOTH but the canonical wage_rate_dollars uses Regular only.
wage_rate_inference_rules.step3_pick_most_recent: Sort by Payroll Check Date desc. Use the rate from the most recent Regular row.
wage_rate_inference_rules.step4_flag_multi_rate: If multiple DISTINCT Regular rates seen for the same employee in the window, set multi_rate=True so the orchestrator can alert. (Calibrated 2026-05-16 on Palmetto: zero multi-rate cases across all 12 employees.)
wage_rate_inference_rules.step5_salaried_handling: If an employee has ZERO Regular rows with hours>0 across the entire 2-month window AND has non-zero amounts under other Descriptions, mark is_salaried=True with wage_rate_dollars=null. The orchestrator/labor-cost calc must exclude these employees from labor% (and they're typically also in store_profile.excluded_from_tip_pool_and_labor_pct).
wage_rate_inference_rules.step6_palmetto_specifics: Lindsay Krause IS paid hourly at $25/hr in this account (not salaried), so is_salaried=False for her too. Exclusion from labor% is by NAME via the store profile's excluded_from_tip_pool_and_labor_pct list, NOT by the salaried flag.

employee_roster_calibration_sample._doc: Snapshot of roster on 2026-05-16. NOT a stable list -- use as parser sanity check only. Production should NOT hardcode names.
notes: Earnings report uses 'LastName, FirstName' format (comma-separated) whereas the Timecard report uses 'LastName FirstName' (space-separated). The two backends produce DIFFERENT raw_name spellings for the same person.

When joining wages with shift hours in the model sheet, normalize via skills/adp_run_automation/name_utils.canonicalize_adp_name() (TODO: create this helper, or apply the same employee_aliases map to both forms).

Active roster is 12 here vs 14 in the Timecard export. The Timecard export over-covers (selected pay periods reach back to Mar 9 which is before some employees' first paycheck in the wages window). Don't assume row count parity.

calibration_session_notes: 2026-05-16 12:30 ET: Live navigation, Cursor AI driving user-playwright. ADP recognized prior session cookies (Keychain-resolved password).

2026-05-16 12:32 ET: User intervened with better navigation -- went straight to Reports > My saved custom reports > Earnings and Hours V1 (we were about to start clicking through the Employees module instead).
2026-05-16 12:33 ET: Set date range 03/22/2026 to 05/15/2026 (BHAGA M1 backfill window). Clicked Preview report; saw the 8-column report shape and confirmed it has everything we need.
2026-05-16 12:35 ET: Downloaded Excel via Download > Excel (.xlsx) > confirmation dialog > Download report. File: Earnings-and-Hours-V1.xlsx, 10516 bytes, 122 rows (5 metadata + 1 header + 116 data).

2026-05-16 12:36 ET: Parsed XLSX with openpyxl. 12 employees, descriptions = {Regular: 57, CC Tips Owed: 25, Cash tips: 16, Misc reimbursement: 12, Overtime: 5, Bonus: 1}. All 11 line-staff @ $15.25/hr; Lindsay Krause @ $25/hr (hourly, not salaried).
